about.description: A multi-platform (Web/PWA/Mobile/Desktop) vocabulary and article practice app built with React + Redux + Tailwind + React Router.
about.wordPracticeDesc: Typing, review, dictation & SM-2 smart scheduling
about.dictManagementDesc: Favorites, wrong words, mastered & custom dictionaries

studyGuide.sm2Intro: SmileX Dict uses the SM-2 (SuperMemo 2) algorithm as its core memory scheduling engine. Developed by Piotr Woźniak in 1987, it is one of the most widely used spaced repetition algorithms. Popular memory software like Anki is based on this algorithm or its variants.
studyGuide.principle1: E-Factor (Easiness Factor): Each word has an E-Factor between 1.3 and 2.5, reflecting its difficulty. Higher E-Factor means faster interval growth.
studyGuide.principle2: Adaptive Intervals: First correct → 1 day review, second correct → 6 days, then multiplied by E-Factor.
studyGuide.principle3: Error Reset: Wrong answer lowers E-Factor and resets interval to zero. 5 consecutive correct answers marks as "mastered".
studyGuide.ebbinghausIntro: German psychologist Hermann Ebbinghaus discovered the laws of forgetting through experiments in 1885. Forgetting begins immediately after learning, and the rate is "fast at first, then slow."
studyGuide.ebbinghausCycleDesc: Traditional Ebbinghaus method suggests reviewing at: 5min → 30min → 12h → 1d → 2d → 4d → 7d → 15d → 30d
studyGuide.sm2Upgrade: SmileX Dict's SM-2 algorithm is an upgraded version of Ebbinghaus' theory. It automatically adjusts review intervals based on your answer quality, making it more efficient than fixed cycles.
studyGuide.activeRecallDesc: Don't just re-read words — actively try to recall them. This "retrieval practice" is 300% more effective than passive reading. When you struggle to recall a word, the memory becomes stronger.
studyGuide.contextLearningDesc: Learning words in context is much more effective than memorizing in isolation. Build English thinking through example sentences and context. Ideal for immersive learning.
studyGuide.spellingMethodDesc: Strengthen muscle memory through typing. The typing mode requires you to input each letter accurately, testing your true spelling knowledge and deepening memory through finger movement. Especially effective for confusing spellings (e.g., -tion/-sion).
studyGuide.spacedRepetitionDesc: SmileX Dict's built-in SM-2 algorithm automatically schedules optimal review times. Just open the app daily and the system will present words due for review. The key is consistent daily practice — even 10-20 words per day yields significant results over time.
studyGuide.spacedRepetitionTip: Auto-scheduled by the system, just practice daily
studyGuide.do1: Study at a fixed time daily (morning or before bed)
studyGuide.do2: 10-30 new words per day, not too many
studyGuide.do3: Complete reviews before learning new words
studyGuide.do5: Pay attention to examples and synonyms
studyGuide.dont1: Cramming too many words at once
studyGuide.dont3: Ignoring reviews, only pursuing new words
studyGuide.dont5: Focusing only on spelling, ignoring pronunciation and usage
studyGuide.dont6: Not analyzing reasons for wrong answers
